title: RapidAPI keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: RapidAPI key detected. RapidAPI keys are authentication credentials used to access thousands of third-party APIs available on the RapidAPI marketplace platform. These keys provide access to paid and free APIs including weather services, social media APIs, financial data providers, machine learning services, geocoding APIs, payment gateways, communication services, and various other external data sources and microservices. Exposing a RapidAPI key allows attackers to make unlimited API calls on your behalf, consume your paid API quotas and credits, access premium API features without authorization, execute expensive API operations that drain your budget, retrieve sensitive data from APIs you've subscribed to, modify or delete data through write-enabled APIs, impersonate your application in API calls, exhaust rate limits preventing legitimate usage, rack up substantial charges on metered APIs, access proprietary business data from specialized APIs, and potentially gain insights into your application's functionality and architecture through API usage patterns. The financial impact can be severe with some premium APIs costing hundreds of dollars per thousand requests. Immediately revoke the exposed RapidAPI key by logging into your RapidAPI dashboard and generating a new key, update all applications and services with the new key, review API usage logs for unauthorized access during the exposure window, check billing statements for unexpected charges from API consumption, contact RapidAPI support if fraudulent charges occurred, implement API usage monitoring and alerting to detect anomalies, rotate keys regularly as a security best practice, use separate API keys for different environments (development, staging, production), implement rate limiting and usage quotas in your applications, store RapidAPI keys in secure environment variables or secret management systems, never commit keys to version control or include in client-side code, audit all subscribed APIs for access during the exposure period, review and revoke access to any APIs no longer needed, implement IP whitelisting if supported by RapidAPI, and document this incident for security audit trails.

RapidAPI key detected in header or variable assignment. RapidAPI is a comprehensive API marketplace that connects developers to thousands of commercial and free APIs across multiple categories including data services, AI/ML, communication, finance, geolocation, and more. API keys from RapidAPI authenticate requests to any subscribed API on their platform. When a RapidAPI key is compromised, attackers can abuse your API subscriptions by making excessive API calls to deplete your usage quotas, access premium features and paid API tiers you're subscribed to, retrieve sensitive business or personal data through data provider APIs, execute write operations in APIs that support data modification, consume expensive metered APIs racking up large bills, access financial APIs to retrieve transaction data or market information, use communication APIs to send unauthorized messages or emails, leverage AI/ML APIs for their own purposes at your expense, exhaust rate limits preventing your legitimate applications from functioning, gain visibility into which APIs your business relies on, and potentially access proprietary data from specialized industry APIs. The financial risk is substantial as some APIs charge per request with costs ranging from cents to dollars per call, and unlimited abuse could result in thousands of dollars in unauthorized charges. Additionally, data accessed through APIs could include customer information, financial records, location data, analytics, or business intelligence depending on your subscriptions. Immediately take action to revoke the compromised key by logging into your RapidAPI account dashboard, navigate to the 'My Apps' or 'API Keys' section, identify the exposed key and revoke/delete it, generate a new RapidAPI key with a unique name, update all application configurations, environment variables, CI/CD pipelines, and deployment services with the new key, restart all services to load the new credentials, review RapidAPI usage analytics and logs to identify unauthorized API calls during the exposure window, check your RapidAPI billing dashboard for unexpected charges or usage spikes, analyze which specific APIs were accessed during the compromise period, contact RapidAPI support immediately if you detect fraudulent usage or charges, dispute any fraudulent charges with complete evidence, implement monitoring and alerting for unusual API usage patterns, set up budget alerts and usage quotas for each API subscription, use separate RapidAPI keys for development, testing, and production environments, implement IP address restrictions if supported, rotate API keys on a regular schedule (quarterly recommended), store keys securely in secret management solutions like AWS Secrets Manager, Azure Key Vault, or HashiCorp Vault, never expose keys in client-side JavaScript or mobile apps, use backend proxies to hide API keys from end users, review and minimize API subscriptions to only those actively needed, implement request validation and rate limiting in your application layer, enable detailed logging for all RapidAPI requests, and maintain an incident response plan for key compromise scenarios.
